name: 10_Face_Recognition_FaceNet_LFW_DL
query: Ayúdame a crear un proyecto de reconocimiento facial en PyTorch usando el modelo FaceNet con el conjunto de datos LFW. Carga el conjunto de datos en `src/model.py`. Obtén el modelo de Hugging Face (puedes encontrarlo en https://huggingface.co/py-feat/facenet) y guárdalo en `models/saved_models/`. Asegúrate de que los datos estén preprocesados para garantizar la estandarización de las imágenes faciales en `src/data_loader.py`. Usa las incrustaciones faciales en `src/model.py` para mejorar el rendimiento de tu sistema. Imprime la precisión del reconocimiento y guárdala en `results/metrics/recognition_accuracy.txt`. Luego, visualiza los resultados de las incrustaciones y guárdalos como `results/figures/embedding_visualization.png`. El modelo debería cargarse sin problemas, idealmente con algún manejo de errores si algo sale mal. Las visualizaciones deberían facilitar la comprensión de cómo las incrustaciones representan características faciales distintas.

requirements.criteria: El conjunto de datos "LFW" (Labeled Faces in the Wild) se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.
La alineación y estandarización de las imágenes faciales se realiza en `src/data_loader.py`.
Se utiliza el modelo "FaceNet" en "PyTorch", cargándolo desde [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/py-feat/facenet). Guarda el modelo en models/saved_models/.
Se utilizan las incrustaciones faciales en `src/model.py`.
La precisión del reconocimiento se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/recognition_accuracy.txt`.
Los resultados de las incrustaciones se visualizan y guardan como `results/figures/embedding_visualization.png`.

preferences.criteria: El proceso de carga del modelo debería ser fluido, con un manejo claro de cualquier problema si el modelo no se carga.
Las visualizaciones de las incrustaciones deberían ser claras y resaltar efectivamente las características faciales distintas.

hint: The page https://huggingface.co/py-feat/facenet provides guidance on how to use FaceNet; however, Hugging Face does not currently offer a model entry for direct use.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "LFW" (Labeled Faces in the Wild) se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file does not actually load the LFW dataset. It only contains a placeholder `load_dataset()` function that returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` with no real dataset loading logic. There is no use of any LFW-specific library (e.g., `sklearn.datasets.fetch_lfw_people`, `torchvision.datasets.LFWPeople`, or any HTTP/file-based download of the LFW dataset). The file merely lists LFW as a keyword string in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` and `KEYWORDS`, which is not functional implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: La alineación y estandarización de las imágenes faciales se realiza en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file contains only a minimal placeholder implementation. The `build_transforms()` function returns a simple list of strings `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]` — there is no actual image alignment or standardization logic implemented. No real facial alignment (e.g., landmark detection, affine transformation) or standardization (e.g., pixel normalization, resizing to fixed dimensions) is performed. The file explicitly states it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" and the `load_dataset()` function simply returns a hardcoded dictionary. There is no functional code that processes facial images in any way.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utiliza el modelo "FaceNet" en "PyTorch", cargándolo desde [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/py-feat/facenet). Guarda el modelo en models/saved_models/.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The code in `src/model.py` is a minimal placeholder that does not implement FaceNet at all. It contains no import or loading of the FaceNet model from Hugging Face (`py-feat/facenet`), no PyTorch model instantiation, and no logic to save the model to `models/saved_models/`. The `build_model()` function simply returns a string placeholder. There is no `models/saved_models/` directory in the project structure, and the trajectory evidence confirms no relevant steps were taken to load or save the FaceNet model.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utilizan las incrustaciones faciales en `src/model.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/model.py` file does not implement facial embeddings. It contains only a placeholder/stub implementation with a `build_model()` function that returns a string ("model placeholder") and a `requirement_notes()` function listing criteria. There is no actual FaceNet model, no embedding extraction logic, no neural network layers, and no code that computes or processes facial embeddings. The file is explicitly described as a "minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" rather than a real implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: La precisión del reconocimiento se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/recognition_accuracy.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/metrics/recognition_accuracy.txt` exists but contains only a placeholder value: `('placeholder\r\n', None)`. This indicates the file was created as a stub but no actual recognition accuracy was computed or saved. There is no evidence in the trajectory of the model being trained, evaluated, or producing real accuracy metrics. The content does not represent a valid recognition accuracy result.

judge_stats.criteria: Los resultados de las incrustaciones se visualizan y guardan como `results/figures/embedding_visualization.png`.

reason: <SATISFIED> The file `results/figures/embedding_visualization.png` exists in the project structure at the expected path `results\figures\embedding_visualization.png`, as confirmed by the workspace tree. The file was generated and saved to the correct location. The error shown in the file content is merely a reading/display error from the evaluation system (related to image format detection during base64 encoding), not an indication that the file is missing or was not created.
